<a id="class-shapecast3d"></a>

# ShapeCast3D

**Inherits:** [Node3D](class_node3d.md#class-node3d) **<** [Node](class_node.md#class-node) **<** [Object](class_object.md#class-object)

A 3D shape that sweeps a region of space to detect [CollisionObject3D](class_collisionobject3d.md#class-collisionobject3d)s.

## Description

Shape casting allows to detect collision objects by sweeping its [shape](#class-shapecast3d-property-shape) along the cast direction determined by [target_position](#class-shapecast3d-property-target-position). This is similar to [RayCast3D](class_raycast3d.md#class-raycast3d), but it allows for sweeping a region of space, rather than just a straight line. **ShapeCast3D** can detect multiple collision objects. It is useful for things like wide laser beams or snapping a simple shape to a floor.

Immediate collision overlaps can be done with the [target_position](#class-shapecast3d-property-target-position) set to `Vector3(0, 0, 0)` and by calling [force_shapecast_update()](#class-shapecast3d-method-force-shapecast-update) within the same physics frame. This helps to overcome some limitations of [Area3D](class_area3d.md#class-area3d) when used as an instantaneous detection area, as collision information isn't immediately available to it.

**Note:** Shape casting is more computationally expensive than ray casting.

## Property Descriptions

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-collide-with-areas"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **collide_with_areas** = `false` [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-collide-with-areas)

-  **set_collide_with_areas**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_collide_with_areas_enabled**()

If `true`, collisions with [Area3D](class_area3d.md#class-area3d)s will be reported.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-collide-with-bodies"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **collide_with_bodies** = `true` [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-collide-with-bodies)

-  **set_collide_with_bodies**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_collide_with_bodies_enabled**()

If `true`, collisions with [PhysicsBody3D](class_physicsbody3d.md#class-physicsbody3d)s will be reported.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-collision-mask"></a>

[int](class_int.md#class-int) **collision_mask** = `1` [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-collision-mask)

-  **set_collision_mask**(value: [int](class_int.md#class-int))
- [int](class_int.md#class-int) **get_collision_mask**()

The shape's collision mask. Only objects in at least one collision layer enabled in the mask will be detected. See [Collision layers and masks](../tutorials/physics/physics_introduction.html#collision-layers-and-masks) in the documentation for more information.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-collision-result"></a>

[Array](class_array.md#class-array) **collision_result** = `[]` [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-collision-result)

- [Array](class_array.md#class-array) **get_collision_result**()

Returns the complete collision information from the collision sweep. The data returned is the same as in the [PhysicsDirectSpaceState3D.get_rest_info()](class_physicsdirectspacestate3d.md#class-physicsdirectspacestate3d-method-get-rest-info) method.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-debug-shape-custom-color"></a>

[Color](class_color.md#class-color) **debug_shape_custom_color** = `Color(0, 0, 0, 1)` [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-debug-shape-custom-color)

-  **set_debug_shape_custom_color**(value: [Color](class_color.md#class-color))
- [Color](class_color.md#class-color) **get_debug_shape_custom_color**()

The custom color to use to draw the shape in the editor and at run-time if **Visible Collision Shapes** is enabled in the **Debug** menu. This color will be highlighted at run-time if the **ShapeCast3D** is colliding with something.

If set to `Color(0.0, 0.0, 0.0)` (by default), the color set in [ProjectSettings.debug/shapes/collision/shape_color](class_projectsettings.md#class-projectsettings-property-debug-shapes-collision-shape-color) is used.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-enabled"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **enabled** = `true` [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-enabled)

-  **set_enabled**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_enabled**()

If `true`, collisions will be reported.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-exclude-parent"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **exclude_parent** = `true` [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-exclude-parent)

-  **set_exclude_parent_body**(value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool))
- [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **get_exclude_parent_body**()

If `true`, the parent node will be excluded from collision detection.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-margin"></a>

[float](class_float.md#class-float) **margin** = `0.0` [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-margin)

-  **set_margin**(value: [float](class_float.md#class-float))
- [float](class_float.md#class-float) **get_margin**()

The collision margin for the shape. A larger margin helps detecting collisions more consistently, at the cost of precision.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-max-results"></a>

[int](class_int.md#class-int) **max_results** = `32` [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-max-results)

-  **set_max_results**(value: [int](class_int.md#class-int))
- [int](class_int.md#class-int) **get_max_results**()

The number of intersections can be limited with this parameter, to reduce the processing time.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-shape"></a>

[Shape3D](class_shape3d.md#class-shape3d) **shape** [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-shape)

-  **set_shape**(value: [Shape3D](class_shape3d.md#class-shape3d))
- [Shape3D](class_shape3d.md#class-shape3d) **get_shape**()

The shape to be used for collision queries.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-property-target-position"></a>

[Vector3](class_vector3.md#class-vector3) **target_position** = `Vector3(0, -1, 0)` [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-property-target-position)

-  **set_target_position**(value: [Vector3](class_vector3.md#class-vector3))
- [Vector3](class_vector3.md#class-vector3) **get_target_position**()

The shape's destination point, relative to this node's [Node3D.position](class_node3d.md#class-node3d-property-position).

---

## Method Descriptions

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-add-exception"></a>

 **add_exception**(node: [CollisionObject3D](class_collisionobject3d.md#class-collisionobject3d)) [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-add-exception)

Adds a collision exception so the shape does not report collisions with the specified node.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-add-exception-rid"></a>

 **add_exception_rid**(rid: [RID](class_rid.md#class-rid)) [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-add-exception-rid)

Adds a collision exception so the shape does not report collisions with the specified [RID](class_rid.md#class-rid).

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-clear-exceptions"></a>

 **clear_exceptions**() [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-clear-exceptions)

Removes all collision exceptions for this shape.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-force-shapecast-update"></a>

 **force_shapecast_update**() [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-force-shapecast-update)

Updates the collision information for the shape immediately, without waiting for the next `_physics_process` call. Use this method, for example, when the shape or its parent has changed state.

**Note:** Setting [enabled](#class-shapecast3d-property-enabled) to `true` is not required for this to work.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-get-closest-collision-safe-fraction"></a>

[float](class_float.md#class-float) **get_closest_collision_safe_fraction**()  [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-closest-collision-safe-fraction)

Returns the fraction from this cast's origin to its [target_position](#class-shapecast3d-property-target-position) of how far the shape can move without triggering a collision, as a value between `0.0` and `1.0`.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-get-closest-collision-unsafe-fraction"></a>

[float](class_float.md#class-float) **get_closest_collision_unsafe_fraction**()  [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-closest-collision-unsafe-fraction)

Returns the fraction from this cast's origin to its [target_position](#class-shapecast3d-property-target-position) of how far the shape must move to trigger a collision, as a value between `0.0` and `1.0`.

In ideal conditions this would be the same as [get_closest_collision_safe_fraction()](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-closest-collision-safe-fraction), however shape casting is calculated in discrete steps, so the precise point of collision can occur between two calculated positions.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-get-collider"></a>

[Object](class_object.md#class-object) **get_collider**(index: [int](class_int.md#class-int))  [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collider)

Returns the collided [Object](class_object.md#class-object) of one of the multiple collisions at `index`, or `null` if no object is intersecting the shape (i.e. [is_colliding()](#class-shapecast3d-method-is-colliding) returns `false`).

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-get-collider-rid"></a>

[RID](class_rid.md#class-rid) **get_collider_rid**(index: [int](class_int.md#class-int))  [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collider-rid)

Returns the [RID](class_rid.md#class-rid) of the collided object of one of the multiple collisions at `index`.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-get-collider-shape"></a>

[int](class_int.md#class-int) **get_collider_shape**(index: [int](class_int.md#class-int))  [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collider-shape)

Returns the shape ID of the colliding shape of one of the multiple collisions at `index`, or `0` if no object is intersecting the shape (i.e. [is_colliding()](#class-shapecast3d-method-is-colliding) returns `false`).

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-get-collision-count"></a>

[int](class_int.md#class-int) **get_collision_count**()  [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collision-count)

The number of collisions detected at the point of impact. Use this to iterate over multiple collisions as provided by [get_collider()](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collider), [get_collider_shape()](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collider-shape), [get_collision_point()](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collision-point), and [get_collision_normal()](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collision-normal) methods.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-get-collision-mask-value"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **get_collision_mask_value**(layer_number: [int](class_int.md#class-int))  [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collision-mask-value)

Returns whether or not the specified layer of the [collision_mask](#class-shapecast3d-property-collision-mask) is enabled, given a `layer_number` between 1 and 32.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-get-collision-normal"></a>

[Vector3](class_vector3.md#class-vector3) **get_collision_normal**(index: [int](class_int.md#class-int))  [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collision-normal)

Returns the normal of one of the multiple collisions at `index` of the intersecting object.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-get-collision-point"></a>

[Vector3](class_vector3.md#class-vector3) **get_collision_point**(index: [int](class_int.md#class-int))  [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-get-collision-point)

Returns the collision point of one of the multiple collisions at `index` where the shape intersects the colliding object.

**Note:** This point is in the **global** coordinate system.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-is-colliding"></a>

[bool](class_bool.md#class-bool) **is_colliding**()  [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-is-colliding)

Returns whether any object is intersecting with the shape's vector (considering the vector length).

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-remove-exception"></a>

 **remove_exception**(node: [CollisionObject3D](class_collisionobject3d.md#class-collisionobject3d)) [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-remove-exception)

Removes a collision exception so the shape does report collisions with the specified node.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-remove-exception-rid"></a>

 **remove_exception_rid**(rid: [RID](class_rid.md#class-rid)) [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-remove-exception-rid)

Removes a collision exception so the shape does report collisions with the specified [RID](class_rid.md#class-rid).

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-resource-changed"></a>

 **resource_changed**(resource: [Resource](class_resource.md#class-resource)) [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-resource-changed)

**Deprecated:** Use [Resource.changed](class_resource.md#class-resource-signal-changed) instead.

This method does nothing.

---

<a id="class-shapecast3d-method-set-collision-mask-value"></a>

 **set_collision_mask_value**(layer_number: [int](class_int.md#class-int), value: [bool](class_bool.md#class-bool)) [🔗](#class-shapecast3d-method-set-collision-mask-value)

Based on `value`, enables or disables the specified layer in the [collision_mask](#class-shapecast3d-property-collision-mask), given a `layer_number` between 1 and 32.
